target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Channel echo / session pinning — with native streaming echo

Mirror a session's turns to additional pinned channels ("echo targets"), so a
conversation that lives in one place can be followed (and continued) from another.
Works across surfaces — a turn from Telegram, the web app, the control UI, or the CLI
mirrors to the pinned target(s), because every surface shares the same session key.

### What gets echoed, and how

- **Prompt** → mirrored with a `📱 [via <channel>]` marker (it genuinely originated
  elsewhere, so the marker is honest).
- **Response** → delivered **natively** on each pinned channel:
  - **Native-final (A):** the response mirrors un-prefixed, so it reads as a real reply
    (not a tagged `[echo]` copy). `fireEchoDeliveries` gained a `{ prefixed: false }` opt-out;
    both echo paths (the `message:sent` hook and the `chat.send` web/CLI path) use it.
  - **Native streaming (B-full):** when a pinned channel has a streaming renderer and
    streaming is enabled, the response renders **live** on that channel — the single
    agent run is replayed into the channel's own draft, editing one message as tokens
    arrive (no second agent run). Telegram ships the first renderer.

**Coexistence, not replacement.** The post-hoc final-text mirror is retained as the
**fallback**: it serves stream-off targets and channels without a renderer yet (only
Telegram has one), and reconciles a dropped partial. A target handled by a live renderer
is gated out of the post-hoc mirror (per-run handled marks) so it never double-delivers.

### Mechanism (no compositor extraction, no N agent runs)

The origin runs the agent **once**; its streaming events already broadcast on the
agent-event bus by `runId`. A per-target **mirror resolver** (`echo-mirror-resolver.ts`)
replays that bus into the target channel's own `on*` callbacks; the **fan-out**
(`echo-streaming.ts`) launches one resolver + one channel renderer per streaming target
at run start, and gates the post-hoc mirror for handled targets. Telegram's renderer
(`extensions/telegram/echo-renderer.ts`) drives the same `createTelegramDraftStream`
primitive the native compositor uses. Channels register via the
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-echo` surface (first-wins, documented ownership contract).

### Tests

Focused coverage now includes echo target resolution + delivery, mirror resolver
replay/ordering/isolation, fan-out + registry + per-run gating, Telegram renderer and
client-options wiring, gateway `chat.send` user echo handling, plugin-sdk subpath
contracts, and duplicate-delivery suppression.

Commands run locally after the review fixes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer-register.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/echo-renderer.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.proxy.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.send-user-echo.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-mirror-resolver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/echo-streaming.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts
```

Focused Marmorkrebs/Stryker mutation run on the source-reply echo block in
`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` killed 14/19 mutants (73.68%). The survivors were
truthy/optional-chain equivalents; the mutants that would regress delivery options,
filtering, payload, and role handling were killed.

Local ClawSweeper found three concrete issues during review and each is addressed in this
branch: source-reply final mirror gating, duplicate `chat.send` prompt echo delivery, and
Telegram echo renderer client-options reuse. A final local rerun after the last fix was
blocked by Codex quota until 2026-06-09 02:30 Europe/London, so remote re-review is
requested after this push rather than claimed as locally cleared.

### Live streaming proof — webchat → Telegram

B-full's live streaming is a **stack-dependency**: the live progress-draft streaming +
the CLI streaming parser come from the persist/commentary PRs, so the *isolated* branch
can't show it. Captured on a **combined build** (this PR + the persist/commentary stack
+ #89851 MCP-loopback transport, 3-way-ported zero-conflict), in a crabbox on a **test
bot** (never the daily driver). A webchat `chat.send` turn pinned to echo into a Telegram
supergroup, response from a deterministic word-by-word model:

```
[fanout] target=telegram:<supergroup> factory=yes renderer=yes
[render] onPartialReply len=3
[render] onPartialReply len=9          # response text grows word-by-word
… 35 streamed deltas → 35 onPartialReply
[finalize] msgId=40 liveEdits=4        # ONE telegram message, EDITED 4x live
telegram outbound send ok messageId=39 # ONLY the prompt echo — no post-hoc duplicate
```

Per turn the pinned chat shows `📱 [via webchat] <prompt>` + the **response message
growing word-by-word** (one message, live-edited), with no duplicate final.

### Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: A webchat turn pinned to a Telegram echo target must mirror the
  prompt with the cross-channel marker, render the assistant response natively on
  Telegram, stream live partials into one edited Telegram message, and avoid a duplicate
  post-hoc final.
- Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw crabbox session on a combined build of this PR
  plus the persist/commentary stack and #89851 MCP-loopback transport, using a test
  Telegram bot and test Telegram supergroup rather than the daily-driver bot.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the Telegram user crabbox proof
  harness (`scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts`) against the combined local build,
  pinned session `agent:main:d1` from webchat to Telegram, sent a deterministic
  word-by-word `chat.send` turn from webchat, and captured the Telegram streaming echo
  runtime log at `.proof-88815/streaming-echo-proof.log` on 2026-06-07.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log excerpt from the real crabbox/webchat to
  Telegram proof run:

```text
2026-06-07T19:39:30.827+00:00 [ECHODBG-launch] sessionKey=agent:main:d1 echoTargets=1
2026-06-07T19:39:30.829+00:00 [ECHODBG-fanout] targets=1 factories=telegram
2026-06-07T19:39:30.833+00:00 [ECHODBG-fanout] target=telegram:<test-supergroup> factory=yes renderer=yes
2026-06-07T19:39:31.207+00:00 [ECHODBG-render] onPartialReply len=3
2026-06-07T19:39:31.550+00:00 [ECHODBG-render] onPartialReply len=9
2026-06-07T19:39:43.626+00:00 [ECHODBG-render] onPartialReply len=216
2026-06-07T19:39:43.991+00:00 [ECHODBG-finalize] finalText.len=216 msgId=40
2026-06-07T19:39:48.760+00:00 [ECHODBG-finalize] after stop msgId=40 liveEdits=4
```

- Observed result after fix: Telegram received the webchat prompt echo and one native
  assistant response message. The assistant response grew via streamed partials to length
  216, finalized on message id 40, and reported `liveEdits=4`; the prompt echo remained
  the only separate outbound delivery, so there was no duplicate final response.
- What was not tested: Daily-driver Telegram bot, non-Telegram streaming renderers, and a
  standalone B-full proof on only this branch. The live streaming proof depends on the
  persist/commentary stack plus #89851; this branch's renderer, fan-out, and duplicate
  gating are covered by focused tests.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The test used a private Telegram test
  supergroup and redacted the group id from the PR body. Maintainer privacy,
  authorization, and operator-warning policy for persistent cross-channel echo targets is
  still intentionally flagged below.

### Open maintainer decisions (flagged by review)

- **Privacy/authorization/operator-warning/docs** for persistent `echoTargets` mirroring
  future session content across transports — a maintainer policy decision before this is a
  supported surface.
- **Public `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` surface** — ownership contract is now documented +
  first-wins; sign-off on it as a stable public subpath is a maintainer call.
  The public export is narrowed to renderer registration plus types.
- Live streaming proof is necessarily on the combined stack (see above); the PR's own code
  (renderer + fan-out + gating, incl. the chat.send + double-delivery fixes) is fully tested.

### Current review state

- Addressed local ClawSweeper findings for source-reply gating, duplicate direct
  `chat.send` prompt echo delivery, public SDK surface leakage, and Telegram client option
  reuse in the echo renderer.
- Next action: remote ClawSweeper re-review on the pushed head.
- Still waiting on maintainer policy call for cross-channel echo privacy/operator warning
  expectations and stable SDK subpath sign-off.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
